params.text:
Welcome to Legionella Awareness

Welcome to Legionella Awareness. Legionella bacteria can grow in the water systems of care homes and other buildings, and in the right conditions they can cause a serious lung infection called Legionnaires' disease. As a care worker you have a small but genuinely important part to play in helping to keep the people you support safe from this risk.

  • Awareness: understanding what legionella is and why it matters.
  • Good practice: the simple everyday habits that help keep water safe.
  • Your role: following the plan, doing assigned tasks and reporting concerns.

What this course covers

Over the next pages you will learn what legionella and Legionnaires' disease are, where the risk comes from in a building's water system, how that risk is controlled, and how to recognise and report problems. The guidance here is based on the Health & Safety Executive's advice and on the principles of the Approved Code of Practice L8 and HSG274 for controlling legionella in water systems.

This e-learning gives you awareness and background knowledge only. It does not replace the hands-on, supervised training your employer provides, and it does not replace your service's legionella risk assessment, which is carried out and managed by the duty holder and the responsible person.
